data.title: Public Storage: Hold Rating Reiterated as Solid Q1 Meets Cautious Outlook; $305 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Juan C. Sanabria maintained a Hold rating on Public Storage today and set a price target of $305.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both near-term strength and tempered forward expectations. Public Storage delivered a better-than-anticipated first quarter, with same-store NOI benefitting from an earlier tax refund and solid move-in trends as discounting eased, which should support a constructive near-term share reaction.

However, he notes that in-place rents are still weakening, and management chose to leave full-year guidance unchanged, signaling caution around the key upcoming leasing season and broader macro uncertainty. Core FFO and same-store revenue guidance remain below both Street and BMO estimates, and management did not revise ancillary or non-same-store growth expectations, leading Sanabria to reaffirm a Hold rating and keep his $305 price target unchanged.

In another report released on April 13, Evercore ISI also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $309.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Lifts Darling Ingredients Price Target to $72, Reaffirming Buy on Structural Edge in Renewable Diesel
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Jason Gabelman maintained a Buy rating on Darling Ingredients today and set a price target of $72.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jason Gabelman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to Darling Ingredients’ positioning in the renewable diesel market and favorable regulatory dynamics. He highlights that the finalized 2026–2027 Renewable Volume Obligation should keep Renewable Identification Number prices high enough to utilize all U.S. biomass-based diesel capacity and attract foreign feedstocks, effectively creating about a $1 per gallon structural edge for the DGD joint venture and supporting higher earnings power.

He projects first-quarter 2026 EBITDA roughly in line with consensus and second-quarter results only modestly softer, while still expecting core profitability to improve as feedstock pricing and DGD margins trend toward $1 per gallon over the year. On this improved outlook and anticipated $0.90 per gallon DGD EBITDA from late 2026 into 2027, he raises his estimates and lifts his price target to $72, reinforcing Darling Ingredients as a top pick in his coverage universe.

In another report released on April 22, Scotiabank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $70.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Surrozen: Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Highlights Wnt-Targeted Ophthalmology Strategy and Maintains $37 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Patrick Dolezal of LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on Surrozen, retaining the price target of $37.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Surrozen’s strategic positioning around Merck’s pivotal BRUNELLO readout and the company’s differentiated bi- and tri-functional Wnt-targeted approach in ophthalmology. He views the two most plausible BRUNELLO efficacy outcomes as either validating the Wnt mechanism or reinforcing the need to pair Wnt agonism with anti-VEGF therapy, both of which would support the value proposition of Surrozen’s SZN-8141 candidate.

He also notes that the downside BRUNELLO scenario of a major safety issue or lack of activity appears unlikely, given encouraging prior AMARONE data and the absence of concerning actions by Merck’s independent monitoring committee. Combined with Surrozen’s focused pipeline strategy in a large, established anti-VEGF market and an unchanged $37 price target, Dolezal concludes that the current risk‑reward profile justifies a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 20, TD Cowen also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $32.25 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Sees Resilient Membership Growth and Manageable Headwinds Supporting Planet Fitness Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Sharon Zackfia has reiterated their bullish stance on PLNT stock, giving a Buy rating on April 24.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sharon Zackfia’s rating is based on the view that Planet Fitness can deliver steady growth despite near-term headwinds, as evidenced by expectations for high single-digit revenue expansion and continued member gains even in a softer comp environment. She attributes temporary pressure to factors such as severe weather and the transition to click-to-cancel, but notes that these issues should ease as the company laps last year’s changes.

At the same time, she highlights ongoing membership growth, disciplined promotional activity that remains comparable year over year, and the ability to modestly grow earnings even with some margin compression from mix shifts like higher advertising revenue. In her assessment, this combination of resilient fundamentals and manageable near-term pressures supports a favorable long-term outlook for Planet Fitness stock, justifying a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 24, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $90.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Bobby Brooks Reiterates Buy on Solaris Energy Infrastructure After Strong Q1 Beat, Contract Momentum; Keeps $81 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Solaris Energy Infrastructure, the Energy sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Bobby Brooks from Northland Securities maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $81.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bobby Brooks has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Solaris Energy Infrastructure’s stronger‑than‑expected first‑quarter performance and robust contract momentum. Revenue and adjusted EBITDA materially surpassed consensus, driven by higher managed megawatts, increased logistics deployments, and better revenue per megawatt, while segment profitability in both Power Solutions and Logistics exceeded forecasts, supporting confidence in cash generation.

Brooks also highlights the newly signed long‑term, investment‑grade 600 MW contract and year‑to‑date contracted volume of 1,100 MW, which still leaves meaningful open capacity and visibility on future growth. With upgraded second‑quarter guidance, an initial third‑quarter outlook, disciplined capital spending below prior expectations, and a solid project and acquisition pipeline, he sees continued upside for SEI shares and reiterates his $81 price target as unchanged.

Brooks covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Tetra Technologies, Graham, and Crane NXT. According to TipRanks, Brooks has an average return of 42.5% and a 71.95% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Morgan Stanley also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $81.00 price target.
